acceptance_criteria: writing a file in a leased-free module auto-acquires exactly one lease
conflict path never blocks the agent and always logs conflict_detected
hooks are fail-soft: tool call succeeds even if the hook errors
installer round-trips settings.json without clobbering user entries
description: New tools/hooks/ambient/: (1) pre-write hook script (Claude Code PreToolUse shape, fail-soft) that resolves the module owning the first written path via sma.gen3.json and runs start:edit automatically when no lease is held — on lease conflict it does NOT block; it records conflict_detected and prints the NEED-YOU line; (2) post-write hook appending a context event (files, intent from the task title env var if present). Installer: node tools/install-agent-skills.mjs --hooks writes the settings.json entries. Explicit mode keeps working unchanged; docs page explains both.
title: Ambient auto-lease/auto-context hooks

acceptance_criteria: --auto produces a non-empty accurate summary on a scripted edit session
selftest updated
description: sma-end-edit.mjs gains --auto: derive the closing context summary from the dirty-baseline delta (files changed, gates run from shell history file if present) so agents can close leases with one flag; falls back to --intent when provided.
title: end:edit --auto summary from dirty delta

acceptance_criteria: the named failure mode has a test that fails before the fix and passes after
module gate green
description: P8.5 r2: fail-soft is right for hook ERRORS, wrong for CONFIRMED conflicts — when the pre-write hook positively detects another live lease on the target, it must block the write (exit 2 per Claude hook contract) instead of proceeding; hook errors stay fail-open.
title: Ambient hooks: fail closed on confirmed live lease conflicts

acceptance_criteria: the named failure mode has a test that fails before the fix and passes after
module gate green
description: BLOCKER (P8.5 r2): registry/active-leases.generated.json is read-modify-write without file locking; two concurrent start:edit calls can drop each other's leases. Implement atomic mutation (lockfile via mkdir/O_EXCL or rename-swap with retry), owner-check on release, and a concurrency selftest spawning parallel acquires that must all survive.

evidence.output_tail: OK sma-lease atomicity selftest (8 distinct + 4 contested; owner-safe release; stale recovery; 645 parse checks)

title: Atomic owner-safe locking for concurrent lease registry mutations
